title: Second script model (Cyrillic) + SOURCES.md
context: M2 proves the per-script playbook on non-Latin scripts with mandatory script-literate review (medium risk tier). Cyrillic is the primary candidate second script, serving Slavic-language learners and adult-literacy programs in Eastern Europe and Central Asia. Wrong stroke order or letter forms in Cyrillic would harm learners whose script is already underserved by free materials. A gate-verified open Cyrillic font and a confirmed script-literate reviewer are blocking dependencies.
objective: Model all Cyrillic letters and digits required for a complete print practice set as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef, commit SOURCES.md with full provenance and snapshots, and obtain script-literate reviewer sign-off on the per-glyph checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: All Cyrillic glyphs (uppercase and lowercase letters for the target language variant, plus digits 0–9 if not already covered by Latin model) modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef.
Gate-verified open Cyrillic font sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact attached); SOURCES.md committed with name, URL, license, retrieval date, snapshot SHA-256 + Wayback URL.
Script-literate reviewer (native/fluent Cyrillic writer or qualified teacher) signs the per-glyph checklist confirming stroke order, direction, and letter forms match the cited authoritative source.
Sheets render in A4 and US Letter with the script-appropriate guideline system; provenance-gate green.
Model committed CC-BY-4.0 (or CC-BY-SA-4.0 if a share-alike source is used, kept separable); every sheet footer carries attribution and license text.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M2 (second script, candidate Cyrillic), section 8 (medium risk — script-literate reviewer mandatory)
output: data/cyrillic/ directory with schema-valid GlyphModel JSON files, SOURCES.md with complete provenance records, reviewer-signed per-glyph checklist, and generated trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0.
